question: What is the strongest caller-facing reading of API2 JSONP callback after combining same-day anonymous value-space probes, callback contract probes, and real anonymous visitor-cookie replay?
scope: Compact callback summary for API2 JSONP auxiliary-parameter closure
contract_reading: Repeated callback behaves as first-value-wins in the tested same-day anonymous JSONP collision cases.
The same repeated-callback precedence stays stable in the tested PC Web and Mobile H5 real anonymous visitor-cookie replay environments.
Wrong-appkey JSONP error shells still honor callback wrapper changes in the same tested environment set.
replay_reading: A full-semantics anonymous real-cookie replay with callback-tail forwarding did not reveal a new callback-dependent parameter branch beyond the existing anonymous baseline.
In that replay scope, empty callback still falls back to `QZOutputJson`, while `a(`, `((`, and `})();` stay in the parse-breaking family.
verdict: The current best reading is wrapper-level, not payload-level: callback can change wrapper prefix and parseability without proving a different backend payload branch.
The strongest caller-facing callback closure now combines value-space coverage, precedence/error-shell behavior, and anonymous real-cookie replay stability in one evidence line.
This is still not callback grammar closure and still not cross-session closure.
formal_verdict: Use callback only with `otype=json` and only when you intentionally need custom JSONP wrapper control.
Do not infer that a custom callback unlocks a richer payload branch; current evidence only supports wrapper control plus a mixed raw-passthrough / parse-breaking value-space.
The next high-value callback closure step is aged-cookie or login-state replay, not another generic anonymous rerun.
remaining_gaps_refined: Aged-cookie replay for callback precedence and wrong-appkey error-shell behavior
Real login-state replay for callback precedence and wrong-appkey error-shell behavior
Broader parser-breaking families beyond the current representative set
Any session-coupled callback behavior outside the current anonymous and anonymous-real-cookie replay scope
